name: Santa Cookie Wreath
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 375°F.
2. In a medium bowl, mix gingerbread mix, hot water, flour, and margarine until dough forms. Set aside one fourth of the dough.
3. Roll remaining dough into a 9-9/16-inch circle, about 1/4-inch thick. Cut out a 4-inch circle from the center to form a wreath shape. Add any dough cut from the center back to the reserved dough.
4. Place the wreath on an ungreased cookie sheet and bake for 15 minutes or until edges are firm. Do not overbake! Let cool for 1 minute before removing from the cookie sheet.
5. Roll the reserved dough into a 1/4-inch thick rectangle. Cut with a 4-inch holly-leaf shaped cookie cutter. Place on an ungreased cookie sheet and bake for 9 minutes, until edges are firm. Cool for 1 minute before removing from the cookie sheet.
6. Melt candy coating over low heat until melted.
7. For each Santa, dip one end of a peanut butter cookie into the coating to resemble Santa's beard. Let dry on a waxed paper-lined cookie sheet.
8. Once the first coat is dry, dip the other end into the coating (leaving the center of the cookie plain for the face). Immediately sprinkle red sugar over the wet coating, leaving about 1/4-inch of coating plain at the cookie's center to resemble the trim of the hat.
9. Cut mini marshmallows into fourths. Place a marshmallow piece on one side of the hat to resemble a pom-pom.
10. Attach two chocolate chips to the cookie using the coating for the eyes.
11. Attach one cinnamon candy to the cookie, using the coating, for the nose.
12. Use remaining candies to decorate holly leaves.
13. Attach Santas and holly leaves to the wreath, using the coating.
